title: Slope and Intercept
concept: A line is defined by two knobs: the intercept is the starting height, and the slope is the steepness.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What is the purpose of the line of best fit?

options.text: To get as close as possible to all data points on average
To connect every dot in chronological order
To filter out all outliers automatically

exercises.explain: Correct! The line of best fit represents the general trend by getting as close to all points as possible.

cards.title: Two control knobs
cards.body: If you want to position a straight line on a graph, you don't need infinite settings. You only need two control knobs to draw any straight line in existence.

cards.title: Slope and intercept
cards.body: The two knobs are the intercept (where the line starts on the vertical axis when the input is 0) and the slope (how much the line rises or falls for each step to the right).

cards.title: Starting height and steepness
cards.body: Think of hiking a trail. The intercept is the elevation of the trailhead where you start. The slope is how steep the trail climbs for every mile you walk forward.

cards.title: Knobs in action
cards.body: In a taxi fare formula, the base fee (e.g. $3.00 just to get in) is the intercept. The price per mile (e.g. $2.00) is the slope. Together, they define the line: Fare = 3 + 2 * Miles.

cards.title: Slopes can be negative
cards.body: A slope doesn't have to be positive (pointing up). If your model predicts car value based on age, the value goes down as age increases. This is represented by a negative slope.

cards.title: Slope vs. Intercept
cards.body: Understanding the role of each knob:

left.text:
Starting height
Value of Y when X is 0
Shifts line up or down

right.text:
Steepness or rate
Change in Y per unit X
Tilts the line's angle

exercises.prompt: In the line equation y = mx + b, what does the intercept b represent?

options.text: The starting value of y when x is zero
The total error of the model

exercises.explain: The intercept is the starting height where the line crosses the vertical y-axis (when x is 0).

exercises.prompt: True or false: A negative slope means that as the input variable increases, the predicted output decreases.
exercises.explain: True. A negative slope tilts the line downward, indicating an inverse relationship.

exercises.sentence: If a taxi charges $5 to start and $2 per mile, the number 2 represents the ___.
exercises.explain: The rate of change per mile ($2) is the slope, which tilts the line of best fit.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following parameters controls the vertical height shift of the entire line without tilting it?
exercises.explain: Changing the intercept shifts the entire line up or down while keeping its angle (slope) the same.

exercises.prompt: A team trains a linear regression model to predict water usage (gallons) in a park based on daily temperature (Fahrenheit). The model learns the formula: Usage = -500 + 40 * Temperature. What does the slope of 40 mean in plain English?

options.text: For every 1 degree increase in temperature, water usage increases by 40 gallons.
The park always starts each day using exactly 40 gallons of water.
When the temperature is 0 degrees, water usage is 40 gallons.
The model made 40 incorrect predictions during training.

exercises.explain: Spectacular! The slope tells you the rate of change: for every single unit increase in the input (1 degree), the output goes up by the slope amount (40 gallons). Now, let's learn how the model measures its mistakes for each individual point!
